channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Veteran Tanzanian Banker and Leader Idris Rashidi Passes Away at 78
item.description: Idris Rashidi, the fourth governor of the Bank of Tanzania, has passed away at the age of 78 on the night of Thursday, March 12, 2026, after battling cancer. He was born in 1948 in Mbulu District, Man...

item.title: Wolfgang Puck reveals 2026 Oscars Governors Ball menu and addresses Hollywood's weight-loss trends
item.description: Celebrity chef Wolfgang Puck, who has catered the Governors Ball – the official afterparty for the Academy Awards – for the 32nd consecutive year, shared details of the 2026 menu with the Daily Mail. ...

item.title: Insights from Bob Winnel on Tackling Canberra’s Housing Shortage
item.description: Bob Winnel, founder of Village Building Co, is stepping down after nearly 50 years in the construction and property development industry. Known for his commitment to affordable housing, Winnel has con...

item.title: Mother of Injured Tumbler Ridge Girl Overwhelmed by Mail Support Requests a Pause
item.description: The mother of 12-year-old Maya Gebala, who survived the Tumbler Ridge school shooting in British Columbia, has asked the public to pause sending letters and packages to their family. Cia Edmonds share...

item.title: Ennerdale Residents Clash with Police During Shack Demolition Operation
item.description: On Thursday in Ennerdale, south of Johannesburg, an operation to demolish unoccupied shacks escalated into a violent confrontation. Johannesburg Metro Police officers from Region G and the by-law mana...

item.title: ASEAN Strengthens Efforts to Protect Citizens in Middle East Conflicts
item.description: The Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) emphasized closer coordination and timely information-sharing among member countries to ensure the safety and evacuation of nationals caught in confl...

item.title: Digital Tools Helping Clinics Manage Patient Attendance and Reduce Missed Dental Visits
item.description: Missed dental appointments are a common challenge that disrupts clinic operations and causes financial losses. The article explains how modern dental management programs are transforming this situatio...

item.title: Claudia Winkleman debuts her BBC One chat show with A-list guests
item.description: Claudia Winkleman has taken a bold step from hosting The Traitors to fronting her own late-night chat show on BBC One, taking over Graham Norton's usual slot for a seven-episode run. The 54-year-old p...

item.title: Nigeria Introduces Lenacapavir Injection for Twice-Yearly HIV Prevention
item.description: Nigeria don start to dey use Lenacapavir, one new long-acting HIV prevention injection wey you only need take twice a year. Di Coordinating Minister of Health and Social Welfare, Ali Pate, don approve...

item.title: Court Hears About Prepaid School Fees of $760,910 for Yahaya Bello’s Children
item.description: For the ongoing trial of former Kogi State Governor Yahaya Bello over alleged ₦110.4 billion fraud, court witnesses have given details of huge sums paid for his children's education. One key witness, ...

item.title: Bullara Station: A Rustic Outback Stay Showcased on Home And Away
item.description: Bullara Station, a 100,000-hectare cattle property between Coral Bay and Exmouth in Western Australia, has been a local favourite for over 16 years, offering camping, caravans, and rustic accommodatio...

item.title: Atmospheric river expected to bring heavy rain and flooding risk to coastal B.C.
item.description: A strong atmospheric river is forecast to affect coastal British Columbia beginning midday Sunday, March 15, coinciding with the start of spring break. Meteorologists warn that this system could conti...

item.title: Families of Apartheid Victims Respond to Legal Challenges in TRC Investigation
item.description: Families of victims of apartheid-era crimes are set to address the media regarding ongoing legal challenges against the Khampepe Commission of Inquiry, which investigates claims of political interfere...

item.title: Police Officer Abducted and Later Found Murdered in Bajaur District
item.description: In Bajaur district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, a tragic incident occurred where 54-year-old police officer Shah Munir Khan, who was on leave, was abducted and later found murdered. The incident took place ...

item.title: Mombasa mother questioned in German national's child trafficking investigation
item.description: In Mombasa, Kenyan police have brought in a local mother for questioning over her possible involvement in a child trafficking case linked to a German national. The matter came to light after authoriti...

item.title: India leads discussions as Brics struggles to form consensus on West Asia conflict
item.description: The formation of a unified stance within Brics on the ongoing Iran-US conflict has encountered significant challenges, as some member nations are directly or indirectly involved in West Asia. Iranian ...

item.title: Document reveals first known photograph of Andrew, Mandelson and Epstein together
item.description: The US Department of Justice has released a photograph showing Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Lord Peter Mandelson with convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein. The image, reportedly taken in Martha's Vi...

item.title: Early Data Shows Teen Social Media Ban Has Had Limited Impact
item.description: Three months after Australia introduced the world-first ban on social media for under-16s, initial data suggests the measure has only slightly reduced use among young Australians. According to parenta...

item.title: UN Warns: Gender Equality Still Elusive Worldwide, Legal Barriers Persist for Women
item.description: Di United Nations don yan saye, even after plenty years of advocacy and law reform, no country for di world don reach full equality between men and women. Dis warning na for International Women's Day ...

item.title: Medieval Danish Cemeteries Show the Sick Were Buried Alongside the Healthy
item.description: A new study examining medieval Danish cemeteries challenges the long-held belief that people with stigmatized diseases were socially excluded. Researchers analyzed 939 adult skeletons from five cemete...
